h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner sequentially applies the field-name and value rule to each new column in the worksheet.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor repeats the same instructional move of confirming and prompting for the next column without any adaptive shift.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn and no subsequent adaptive tutor uptake.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn and no subsequent adaptive tutor uptake.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor repeats the same instructional move of confirming and prompting for the next column without any adaptive shift.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor repeats the same instructional move of confirming and prompting for the next column without any adaptive shift.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I keep wanting to write “sales went up,”
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will mechanically fill in the y-axis variables and units exactly as templated by the tutor.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The flagged text is a conclusion phrase, so it waits for the trend sentence.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor addresses the auto-flag by reiterating the prior rule and template rather than inventing a new adaptive mechanism or shifting the cognitive load.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Keep that split. Worksheet standard: printed label first; unit only if printed.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor validates the learner's conceptual summary but simply continues with the same procedural template without adapting the task or framing.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I keep wanting to write “sales went up,”

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Keep that split. Worksheet standard: printed label first; unit only if printed.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor validates the learner's conceptual summary but simply continues with the same procedural template without adapting the task or framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The flagged text is a conclusion phrase, so it waits for the trend sentence.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor addresses the auto-flag by reiterating the prior rule and template rather than inventing a new adaptive mechanism or shifting the cognitive load.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The flagged text is a conclusion phrase, so it waits for the trend sentence.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor addresses the auto-flag by reiterating the prior rule and template rather than inventing a new adaptive mechanism or shifting the cognitive load.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to draw arrows in the direction of the chase due to their entrenched cognitive bias.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: On rabbit-hawk, name the food first, then set the pencil tail there. The head should land on the organism getting the energy, not the one doing the chasing.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a physical object manipulation and a strict sequencing routine to bypass the learner's entrenched 'chasing' heuristic.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: With no pivot learner turn established, there is no recognition-contingent uptake to score.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: With no pivot learner turn established, there is no recognition-contingent uptake to score.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: On rabbit-hawk, name the food first, then set the pencil tail there. The head should land on the organism getting the energy, not the one doing the chasing.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces a physical object manipulation and a strict sequencing routine to bypass the learner's entrenched 'chasing' heuristic.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: On rabbit-hawk, name the food first, then set the pencil tail there. The head should land on the organism getting the energy, not the one doing the chasing.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor introduces a physical object manipulation and a strict sequencing routine to bypass the learner's entrenched 'chasing' heuristic.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner systematically identifies contacts and draws force arrows independent of the travel arrow.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a tutor strategy reversal.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn to which the tutor could adapt.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn to which the tutor could adapt.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a tutor strategy reversal.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a tutor strategy reversal.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues to verify that each contour line represents a single elevation.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a reversal in the tutor's strategy.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: Without a pivot learner turn, there is no revised frame for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: Without a pivot learner turn, there is no revised frame for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a reversal in the tutor's strategy.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: There is no learner resistance or breakdown to trigger a reversal in the tutor's strategy.
